evidence: Docstring: a set X_k meeting every infinite arithmetic progression in V but intersecting every k-element arithmetic progression in at most two points.
Baumgartner's headline statement [Ba75] about sets meeting every infinite AP while meeting 3-term APs in at most two points.
Docstring asks whether a Sidon set can meet every infinite arithmetic progression (IsSidon A in the statement); erdosproblems.com/198 is tagged 'sidon sets'.
Statement quantifies over infinite arithmetic progressions (IsAPOfLength Y ⊤) that a Sidon set must intersect.
AlphaProof's explicit construction {n!+n} is asserted to be a Sidon set meeting every infinite arithmetic progression.
The construction must intersect every infinite arithmetic progression (IsAPOfLength Y ⊤ → (A ∩ Y).Nonempty).
The hypothesis ThreeAPFree A is exactly that A is a progression-free (Salem–Spencer-type) set containing no 3-term arithmetic progression.
Docstring: must the complement of a 3-AP-free set of reals contain an infinite arithmetic progression? (Baumgartner [Ba75] answered no.)
Erdős Problem 20 is the sunflower conjecture — is f(n,k) < c_k^n for the k-sunflower threshold — with the file header citing Wikipedia's Sunflower (mathematics) and [ErRa60].
Docstring: Erdős and Rado [ErRa60] proved the factorial upper bound (k-1)^n n! for the k-sunflower threshold (the sunflower lemma).
Docstring: does the longest arithmetic progression of primes in {1,...,N} have length o(log N)?
The quantity bounded is longestPrimeArithmeticProgressions, the length of the longest arithmetic progression of primes up to n.
Variant: such a progression of primes has length ≤ (1+o(1)) log N.
Docstring: 'It follows from the prime number theorem that such a progression has length ≤ (1+o(1)) log N.'
Docstring: is there m coprime to 6 such that none of 2^k·3^l·m+1 are prime, for any k,l ≥ 0?
erdosproblems.com/203 is tagged 'covering systems' and the file TODO plans covering-system statements — the Sierpiński-style construction behind such m uses covering systems.
Docstring: are there n with a covering system whose moduli are the divisors of n that is 'as disjoint as possible'? (Adenwalla [Ad25] proved no such n exist.)
Docstring: for the sequence s of squarefree numbers, is s_{n+1} - s_n ≪_ε s_n^ε?
Docstring: is the gap between consecutive squarefree numbers ≤ (1+o(1))(π²/6) log(s_n)/log log(s_n)?
Docstring: in [Er79] Erdős suggests perhaps s_{n+1} - s_n ≪ log s_n for the squarefree sequence, but is 'very doubtful'.
Docstring 'Is there a dense subset of ℝ² such that all pairwise distances are rational?' is precisely the Erdős–Ulam problem.

The problem — n plane points in general position (no 3 collinear, no 4 concyclic) with integer pairwise distances — is the finite general-position question surrounding the Erdős–Anning theorem on integer-distance sets.
The Kreisel–Kurz 7-point integer-distance configuration in general position is the best construction for this integer-distance problem related to the Erdős–Anning theorem.
